speaker: Russ
en: Erm ... hello Professor, I'm Russ.

speaker: Russ
en: I spoke to you on the phone last week.

speaker: Tutor
en: Ah, yes, come in and have a seat.

speaker: Tutor
en: You want to talk to me about the archaeology course, right?

speaker: Russ
en: Yes, I've read the handbook, but I still have some questions and want to know more details before deciding whether to do it.

speaker: Tutor
en: Right.

speaker: Tutor
en: So, what would you like to know?

speaker: Russ
en: Well, first of all, when I choose the archaeology course, can I integrate it with other subjects?

speaker: Russ
en: For example, one in anthropology?

speaker: Tutor
en: Yes, it can be combined with any other subjects apart from classical history.

speaker: Tutor
en: That's because there is some overlap in the lecture times for those two courses.

speaker: Tutor
en: We weren't able to coordinate them.

speaker: Russ
en: That's OK.

speaker: Russ
en: Then about the modules, could you tell me the details?

speaker: Tutor
en: In the first semester, there are three modules, all of which are compulsory.

speaker: Russ
en: What about optional modules?

speaker: Tutor
en: Well, we don't offer optional modules until next semester.

speaker: Russ
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: The first module is called Object Matters, and Dr Morris teaches it.

speaker: Tutor
en: This module is mainly about what can be learnt from specific artifacts such as pottery and stone tools.

speaker: Russ
en: That's something I've never heard about.

speaker: Russ
en: I'm interested.

speaker: Russ
en: How is the module presented?

speaker: Russ
en: I mean, is it all lectures?

speaker: Tutor
en: Well, you mean the learning method.

speaker: Tutor
en: In this case, its lectures are combined with practical sessions, so it's a mixture.

speaker: Russ
en: What about the content of the module?

speaker: Russ
en: I suppose we will be looking at different kinds of archaeological remains and how to date them, that kind of thing.

speaker: Tutor
en: To some extent, yes.

speaker: Tutor
en: But the module is based on processes.

speaker: Tutor
en: And it covers four aspects in detail: first of all, recording material, and then classification, then the interpretation of the data, that's how archaeologists conclude their findings, and finally, display.

speaker: Tutor
en: Is that OK for you?

speaker: Russ
en: I think so.

speaker: Russ
en: Yes, thanks!

speaker: Russ
en: Oh, one important thing, how is the module assessed?

speaker: Russ
en: Should we take an exam at the end of the semester?

speaker: Tutor
en: No.

speaker: Tutor
en: It's all based on coursework after each lecture, so try your best when the lecturer assigns you a task.

speaker: Russ
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: The second module's title is Towns and Cities.

speaker: Tutor
en: It is taught by Professor Elliot, the head of our department.

speaker: Tutor
en: And as the name suggests, the module focuses on the origins of built environments and how they developed.

speaker: Russ
en: That's mainly factual, I suppose.

speaker: Tutor
en: It is.

speaker: Tutor
en: And for that reason, it is assessed by examination.

speaker: Tutor
en: But instead of a written exam, we set it as an oral one, as we think most students prefer this way.

speaker: Russ
en: It sounds a bit scary.

speaker: Russ
en: I prefer written exams.

speaker: Tutor
en: Don't worry too much.

speaker: Tutor
en: Most of our students find they enjoy it.

speaker: Russ
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: And then the third module is called Method and Science.

speaker: Tutor
en: And in this module, Dr Thompson will introduce you to the standard techniques used in archaeological fieldwork and analysis, such as excavating and dating.

speaker: Russ
en: What about the learning method for this module?

speaker: Russ
en: Are there any lectures, or it's all laboratory work?

speaker: Tutor
en: Oh, there aren't any lectures.

speaker: Tutor
en: It combines half lab work and half seminars.

speaker: Russ
en: What about the assessment?

speaker: Tutor
en: Oh, you are lucky; this module has no formal assessment.

speaker: Tutor
en: But right at the end of the module, you will have to take part in a site survey.

speaker: Russ
en: That's great!

speaker: Russ
en: Where shall we go for the site survey?

speaker: Tutor
en: Sorry, I can't tell you the location yet.

speaker: Tutor
en: That would be announced later.

speaker: Tutor
en: The date for that is the week beginning the 10th of March.

speaker: Russ
en: OK.

speaker: Russ
en: That's all I want to know.

speaker: Russ
en: Thank you very much, Professor.

speaker: Russ
en: Bye!

speaker: Tutor
en: You are welcome, Russ.

speaker: Tutor
en: Bye!
